Argon2id was chosen for its side-channel resistance (side-channel protection).
Drizzle is used because it provides zero dependencies (zero-deps).
They verify tokens using RS256 and the shared JWKS endpoint (all-svc-JWKS).
Rate limiting is done at the ALB to provide pre-container protection (pre-container).
The chain involves HTTP-API (gw-schema), Lambda-JWKS, ALB, the Portfolio service (FastAPI), and the RBAC-middleware.
All services are affected (all-svc-JWKS). New validations fail, but there is an LRU-cache-grace period for existing validation context.
Real-time functionality depends on WebSockets, which are scheduled for Q3 (next: WebSocket-Q3). Alerting is currently handled by the Notification service (Go/Chi, SES+SNS+FCM).

At rest: AES256GCM for tenant data and Argon2id for credentials. In transit: Envoy-mTLS for internal traffic and sslmode-verify-full for database connections.
The lifecycle is: HTTP-API (gw-schema) -> Lambda-JWKS -> ALB -> handlePost -> Argon2id -> MFA-TOTP -> RS256-tokens.
It must follow the handle{V}{R} style, use RFC7807 for errors, Pino-JSON for logging, testcontainers for testing, and integrate with the RS256-JWKS auth and Envoy-mTLS infra.
The ALB-rate layer would handle rate limiting before it reaches the container. The HTTP-API Gateway also performs schema validation (gw-schema).
The Auth Service (signs), the Portfolio and Notification Services (verify), and the Lambda-JWKS (provides public keys).
